steps.title: Use the migration prediction
steps.content: The researcher's claim predicts a distance pattern: as humans moved farther from Africa, languages should retain fewer sounds from the original language. A finding that weakens the claim would show a region outside Africa with more sounds than languages in Africa, reversing the predicted relationship between distance and sound count.

steps.title: Reject findings that fit or miss it
steps.content: Choice A fits the prediction because Central America is farther from Africa and has fewer sounds than Western Europe. Choice C only discusses consistency within late-settled parts of Africa, and Choice D compares regional variation without showing a reversal by distance. The weakening finding is the one that makes farther regions have more sounds than Africa. The answer is B.
